figures.caption: Figure 7: System design of the proposed ACCNOTE framework.
Figure 9: One example post with three different notes used in user study. Method names are anonymized in the survey.
Figure 10: Statistical results from the user study. Bars indicate mean scores; error bars are 95% CIs.

Table 8: Detailed topic taxonomy for the XCHECK dataset. Subcategories were included in prompts to guide the LLM; any outputs containing subcategories were converted to the parent topics for the final annotations.
Figure 11: The original post for Example 3.
Figure 8: The original post for Example 2, with post text claims this is “President and First Lady of Syria.”

Table 1: Topic taxonomy in the collected XCHECK dataset. D stands for “deceptive”, ND stands for “non-deceptive”. (One data entry may contain multiple topical labels, so the percentage summation may exceed 100%.)
Figure 3: Example data entry in XCHECK, with image, structured post metadata and external context.
